title: Kulfi

ingredients.title: For the Kulfi Base
items: Heavy cream – 100 ml (optional for extra creaminess)
Sugar – 80–100 g (adjust to taste, optional if using sweetened condensed milk)

instruction.title: Step 1: Infuse Milk
steps: In a heavy-bottomed pan, bring the milk to a boil.
Lower the heat and simmer, stirring occasionally to avoid burning.
Add crushed cardamom and saffron strands.

instruction.title: Step 2: Reduce & Thicken
steps: Reduce the milk to about half its original volume (this may take 25–30 minutes).
Add condensed milk and heavy cream; simmer for 5–7 minutes while stirring.

instruction.title: Step 3: Add Nuts & Flavors
steps: Mix in chopped pistachios and almonds.
If making mango kulfi, blend in mango puree after cooling slightly.
For rose kulfi, add rose water at this stage.

instruction.title: Step 4: Cool the Mixture
steps: Let it cool to room temperature.
Strain through a fine sieve for a silky texture.

instruction.title: Step 5: Freeze in Molds
steps: Pour into kulfi molds or small metal cups.
Insert sticks and freeze for 6–8 hours, preferably overnight for best texture.

instruction.title: Step 6: Serve
steps: Unmold kulfi by dipping molds in warm water for 10–15 seconds.
Garnish with chopped pistachios, saffron strands, or edible silver leaf for a restaurant finish.

chefTips: Use full-fat milk and cream for that rich, creamy texture that melts in your mouth.
Slow reduction of milk is key; never rush with high heat.
Straining ensures there are no cardamom husks or lumps, giving a smooth, luxurious finish.
Metal molds help kulfi freeze evenly and develop the classic dense texture, unlike ice cream which is lighter and airier.
